title: Determining start position in three-dimensional printers
abstract: A method includes monitoring a rate of change of an output of a non-contact distance sensor forming part of a three-dimensional printer that includes the non- contact distance sensor, a build plate, and at least one data processor, wherein the non-contact distance sensor is arranged above the build plate and the output of the non-contact distance sensor characterizes a distance between the non-contact distance sensor and the build plate, the build plate configured to be in motion towards the non- contact distance sensor and/or the non-contact distance sensor configured to be in motion towards the build plate; stopping, in response to identifying a decrease in the rate of change of the output of the non-contact distance sensor, the build plate at a stop position; calculating, based on the stop position, a start position for the build plate; and providing the start position. Related apparatus, systems, techniques and articles are also described.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method comprising : monitoring a rate of change of an output of a non-contact distance sensor forming part of a three-dimensional printer that includes the non-contact distance sensor, a build plate, and at least one data processor, wherein the non-contact distance sensor is arranged above the build plate and the output of the non-contact distance sensor characterizes a distance between the non-contact distance sensor and the build plate, the build plate configured to be in motion towards the non-contact distance sensor and/or the non-contact distance sensor configured to be in motion towards the build plate; stopping, in response to identifying a decrease in the rate of change of the output of the non-contact distance sensor, the build plate at a stop position; calculating, using the at least one data processor and based on the stop position, a start position for the build plate; and providing the start position. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the three-dimensional printer includes a nozzle and the decrease in the rate of change of the output indicates that the nozzle has made contact with the build plate. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising: positioning the non-contact distance sensor above the build plate of the three- dimensional printer; raising the build plate toward the non-contact distance sensor and an extruder nozzle; identifying when a rate of change of the non-contact distance sensor reading decreases; and recording a position of the build plate upon stopping as the stop position. 4. The method of claim 1, further comprising positioning the build plate at the calculated start position. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein the build plate is positioned at the calculated 

 start position without operator intervention. 6. The method of claim 1, further comprising issuing a warning in response to determining that the start position indicates the build plate is not installed. 7. The method of claim 1, further comprising issuing a warning in response to determining that the start position indicates the build plate is not properly installed. 8. The method of claim 1, further comprising obtaining a plurality of start positions by repeating the monitoring, the stopping, and the calculating at multiple positions across the build plate. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising calculating a best fit plane from the plurality of start positions. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising calculating an alternate coordinate system with an alternate X and Y axes lying in the calculated best fit plane. 11. The method of claim 10, further comprising building a three-dimensional part in the alternate coordinate system. 12. The method of claim 1, further comprising automatically positioning the build plate at the calculated start position. 13. The method of claim 1, further comprising performing calculations based on the calculated start position to define a coordinate system. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising building a three-dimensional object in the defined coordinate system. 15. The method of claim 1, wherein providing the start position includes transmitting, storing, processing, and/or displaying the start position. 16. The method of claim 1, wherein the three-dimensional printer includes an extruder assembly including an extruder nozzle and a heat source. 17. The method of claim 1, wherein the three-dimensional printer includes a fusion deposition modeling apparatus, a laminated object manufacturing apparatus, and/or a photopolymer deposition apparatus. 18. The method of claim 1, wherein the three-dimensional printer includes a print head and ink delivery system that includes a print head including a thermal print head, a piezo print head, a MEMS print head, an electrostatic print head, a plotter-style single nozzle unit, a continuous ink jet, and/or a drop-on demand system. 19. The method of claim 1, wherein the non-contact distance sensor includes a sonar sensor, capacitive sensor, Linear Variable Differential Transformer (LVDT), photo diode, and/or phototransistor. 20. A system comprising: a build plate; a non-contact distance sensor arranged above the build plate, an output of the non-contact distance sensor characterizes a distance between the non-contact sensor and the build plate, the build plate configured to be in motion towards the non-contact distance sensor and/or the non-contact distance sensor configured to be in motion towards the build plate; and at least one data processor configured to monitor a rate of change of the output of the non-contact distance sensor, stop, in response to identifying a decrease in the rate of change of the output of the non-contact distance sensor, the build plate at a stop position, calculate, based on the stop position, a start position for the build plate, and provide the start position. 21. The system of claim 1, further comprising a nozzle and wherein the decrease in the rate of change of the output indicates that the nozzle has made contact with the build plate. 22. The system of claim 20, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to: position the non-contact distance sensor above the build plate; raise the build plate toward the non-contact distance sensor and a nozzle; identify when a rate of change of the non-contact distance sensor reading decreases; and record a position of the build plate upon stopping as the stop position. 23. The system of claim 20, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to position the build plate at the calculated start position. 24. The system of claim 23, wherein the system is configured to position the build plate at the calculated start position without operator intervention. 25. The system of claim 20, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to issue a warning in response to determining that the start position indicates the build plate is not installed. 26. The system of claim 20, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to issue a warning in response to determining that the start position indicates the build plate is not properly installed. 27. The system of claim 20, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to obtain a plurality of start positions by repeating the monitoring, the stopping, and the calculating at multiple positions across the build plate. 28. The system of claim 27, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to calculate a best fit plane from the plurality of start positions. 29. The system of claim 28, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to calculate an alternate coordinate system with an alternate X and Y axes lying in the calculated best fit plane. 30. The system of claim 29, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to build a three-dimensional part in the alternate coordinate system. 31. The system of claim 20, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to automatically position the build plate at the calculated start position. 32. The system of claim 20, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to perform calculations based on the calculated start position to define a coordinate system. 33. The system of claim 32, wherein the at least one data processor is further configured to build a three-dimensional object in the defined coordinate system. 34. The system of claim 20, wherein providing the start position includes transmitting, storing, processing, and/or displaying the start position. 35. The system of claim 20, further comprising an extruder assembly including extruder nozzle and a heat source. 36. The system of claim 20, further comprising a fusion deposition modeling apparatus, a laminated object manufacturing apparatus, and/or a photopolymer deposition apparatus. 37. The system of claim 20, further comprising a print head and ink delivery system that includes a print head including a thermal print head, a piezo print head, MEMS print head, an electrostatic print head, a plotter-style single nozzle unit, a continuous ink jet, and/or a drop-on demand system. 38. The system of claim 20, wherein the non-contact distance sensor includes a sonar sensor, capacitive sensor, Linear Variable Differential Transformer (LVDT), photo diode, and/or phototransistor. 39. Apparatus, systems, articles, and techniques described or illustrated herein.
